ksa_text: Knowledge of AI and machine learning-specific model risk considerations, including data drift, concept drift, black-box explainability requirements, model instability, and how traditional model risk frameworks must adapt for non-parametric, ensemble, deep learning, and foundation models.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AG-K-008 (AI Governance/Knowledge) scores 0.549 composite (highest: EU AI Act at 0.549). Scope is in_scope for Clinical AI Specialist: 2 in-scope signals, 0 out-of-scope signals. Seniority is appropriate.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with in_scope scope, AI Governance, composite 0.549. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support AI Governance for Clinical AI Specialist?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: AI Governance appears in approximately 80% of Clinical AI Specialist job postings. FDA documentation, model cards, and clinical AI governance appear in 75-85% of postings.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Include upheld. AI Governance is market-validated for Clinical AI Specialist. Final decision: include.
